justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one of four pedestrians and two of five vehicles from the ground truth, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected entities the extraction provides useful attributes (type, motion_status, actions, age, door/trunk states), but it lacks instance coverage and some contextual labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided (next to, behind), but they are sparse and cannot be validated against detailed ground-truth relations, reducing confidence.
justifications.overall: The extraction is informative for the few detected objects but misses a large number of instances, yielding moderate overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian out of four and two vehicles out of five are reported, so the extraction misses the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected entities the model provides several attributes (actions, age, motion, trunk/doors) but many scene actors and contextual details are absent.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction supplies a few plausible spatial relations (e.g., behind the truck) but relations are sparse and not provided for most objects.
justifications.overall: While attribute quality for reported objects is reasonable, the large number of missing pedestrians and vehicles substantially reduces overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Missed both pedestrians and two of five annotated vehicles, detecting only three vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several per-vehicle attributes (type, motion_status, trunk/doors) but omits all pedestrian information and misrepresents object counts.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some generic spatial notes but they do not capture the ground-truth contextual relations such as pedestrians walking on the road.
justifications.overall: Reasonable attribute extraction for detected vehicles but significant omissions of pedestrians and some vehicles undermine overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only one of two pedestrians and two of four vehicles from the ground truth, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some useful attributes are present (actions, age, motion status, trunk/doors) but several vehicles have incorrect or missing motion labels and there are no track IDs, reducing semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations arrays are empty despite textual hints like left and behind, so explicit relational information is largely absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains a few correct attributes but misses multiple actors and relational details, resulting in poor overall fidelity to the ground truth.
